title: 1. Client Overview
content:
- **Business name:** Lankford Roofing & Construction LLC
- **Location / niche:** Roofing contractor. Texas / Oklahoma footprint is implied from on-page phone numbers; exact office/base location was not clearly stated in the project material reviewed.
- **Key contacts:**
  - **Tanatsa Kamhunga** — appears to be the main business contact tied to reporting and strategy conversations. In an email signature shown in project context, Tanatsa is listed as **CEO, Metal Warehouse Inc.** It is clear he is involved with Lankford decision-making, but his exact formal role inside Lankford is **not confirmed**.
- **Website:** `https://lankfordroofing.com/`
- **GBP / social links:** Lankford Roofing & Construction LLC GBP is referenced by name, but no direct GBP URL or social links were surfaced in the project material reviewed.
- **Services offered (explicitly mentioned):**
  - Commercial roofing
  - Standing seam metal roofs / standing seam metal roofing
- **Target areas / locations:**
  - Texas
  - Oklahoma
  - More specific service areas were **not clearly documented** in the accessible project context.

title: 2. Client Persona & Communication Style
content:
- Communication appears **businesslike, direct, and fairly high-level**.
- Tanatsa seems to care about whether important growth levers are already covered, and whether something new is credible or redundant.
- Likely priorities:
  - Visibility / rankings
  - Evidence that work is being handled proactively
  - Practical outcomes rather than theory
- Sensitivities / preferences:
  - Avoid hype without proof.
  - Be ready to explain whether a vendor/tool actually adds value.
  - Keep updates grounded in results, screenshots, or clear before/after evidence.
- Preferred update channel: **email** is clearly documented. Other channels were not confirmed.

title: 3. Timeline of Key Events
content:
- **2026-02-12:** Lankford and Metal Warehouse reports were discussed internally as both “doing well.” An email draft to Tanatsa was being prepared around performance and AI visibility context.
- **Before 2026-02-12:** Tanatsa had also asked for an audit of **Augusta Custom Homes**, which was used as a comparison point. Augusta was framed as having weaker results and weaker AI visibility than Lankford and Metal Warehouse.
- **2026-02-17:** Work focused on creative direction for the **standing seam metal roofing** page and gallery imagery for Lankford. Multiple iterations were discussed for above-the-fold banner imagery and commercial roofing visuals.
- **2026-02-25:** Tanatsa asked whether a third-party Maps/CTR-style vendor (**Jumper Media**) was something already being done. The response prepared was that this angle was already covered internally alongside standard fundamentals.

title: 4. SEO / Delivery History
content:
- Work explicitly discussed:
  - Ongoing reporting/performance review
  - AI visibility discussion / benchmarking
  - Commercial roofing page support, specifically for **standing seam metal roofing**
  - Image direction for website assets / gallery / hero section
  - Maps visibility / CTR-style support was referenced as already being covered
- What appears to have worked:
  - Lankford was described as doing well in reports
  - Lankford reportedly had stronger AI visibility than Augusta Custom Homes in the comparison discussed
- What did not surface clearly:
  - Detailed breakdown of citations, project pages, GBP posts, citations, backlinks, Local Wizz pages, or web dev tickets specific to Lankford
  - Specific ranking tables, traffic deltas, or call metrics
- Current state:
  - Positive overall tone in reporting
  - No exact keyword ranking set or traffic figures were present in the material reviewed
- Tools / platforms mentioned in relation to performance context:
  - **SEMrush** (AI visibility comparison context)
  - A Maps CTR / engagement vendor category was discussed, but no specific in-house tool stack for Lankford was fully documented here

title: 5. Writing Style & Tone Used
content:
- Tone used for Lankford website content direction was:
  - Practical
  - Strong and durable
  - Commercial / industrial
  - Clean rather than fluffy
- Example angle used on-page:
  - “Sleek, durable, and weather-resistant roofing built to last for decades.”
- Creative direction that was favored for imagery:
  - Realistic
  - Edgy / visually strong
  - Drone-captured or zoomed-in steel roof aesthetic
  - Avoid over-detailed, unnatural skyline imagery
- Messaging preference inferred:
  - Emphasize durability, weather resistance, and commercial suitability
  - Keep visuals credible and not overdesigned

title: 6. Ideas Generated (Used or Unused)
content:
- Standing seam metal roofing page enhancements
- New hero / banner imagery for commercial roofing pages
- General commercial roofing gallery imagery
- AI ranking / AI visibility as an upsell or future strategic layer
- Continued handling of Maps CTR / engagement support internally rather than delegating to an outside vendor

title: 7. Results & Wins
content:
- Lankford was described internally as **doing well** in reporting.
- Lankford was said to have **better AI visibility** than Augusta Custom Homes in a SEMrush-based comparison.
- No exact keyword, traffic, call, or GBP movement figures were visible in the project material reviewed.

title: 8. Open Issues / Unfinished Business
content:
- Exact performance numbers for Lankford were not captured in the accessible material and should be pulled from the live reporting stack before strategy decisions.
- If AI ranking / LLM visibility is going to be sold in, that offer positioning appears to have been discussed but not fully formalized in the material reviewed.
- Website image asset production for roofing/gallery pages may still need final execution depending on what was implemented after the prompt work.

title: 9. Anything Else Worth Knowing
content:
- Tanatsa appears to operate across multiple related business entities or at minimum is deeply involved in both Lankford and Metal Warehouse conversations.
- The relationship seems commercially stable, but this account likely responds best to concrete evidence, not generic agency language.
- There is clear openness to newer visibility channels like AI ranking, but credibility matters. Any recommendation should be framed with proof, controlled testing, and a clear distinction from standard SEO.
